      iPhone / iPad
      
                
        
                  
      1. Open the app in Safari.
        2.

        3.

        4. 
                
      
              
      
              
      
                
      Android tablet
      
                
        
                  
      1. Open the app in Chrome.
        2. 
                  
      2. Tap Install App if shown, or Add to Home screen.
        3. 
                  
      3. Use Download HTML for a standalone offline file.
        4.

      Local privacy vault
      
            
      Pick a local workspace
      
            
      No cloud upload by default.
      
            
      No pasted chart text is uploaded to an app server. Vaults save only reviewed de-identified workspace data in this browser; raw notes, admission text, names, MRNs, and room numbers are not persisted.
      
          
      
          
      
            Checking vault
            Looking for encrypted local storage in this browser.

      Saved local vault

      Caps Lock is on.
      
              
      A blank vault starts with Case A. Use non-identifying labels only.
      
              
      
            
      
            
        
              
      • Store an encrypted local census

      One patient, no vault
      
                
      Not saved after this browser session. No roster or encrypted census storage is created.
      
              
      
            
      
            
      Best for a quick one-patient prep when you do not need a saved roster.
      
            
        
              
      • Best for ad-hoc consults or quick prep
        • 
              
      • Everything stays on this device
        • 
              
      • Start immediately
        • 
              
      • No long-term storage
        • 
            
      
            
          
      
        
      

        
      
          Forgot passcode?
          
      
            
      The app cannot recover your passcode. If you cannot unlock this browser's vault, the only local recovery path is to wipe the encrypted vault stored in this browser and start over.
      
            
          
      
        
      

        
      
          Reset / replace vault
          
      
            
      This deletes the encrypted vault stored in this browser. This does not affect OpenEvidence or Epic. It only removes this app's local IndexedDB vault record.
      
            
            
      
              
            
      
            
      
            
      
              
              
      
                
                
              
      
              
      Caps Lock is on.
      
              
            
      
            
      
              
              
            
      
            
            
      
          
      
        
      

        
        
          
      • No app server upload This app does not send pasted chart text, census data, or case workspaces to a backend service by default.
        • 
          
      • De-identified vault records Only reviewed de-identified workspace data is saved; raw chart text and obvious roster identifiers are dropped before vault storage.
        • 
          
      • You control sharing Information leaves this device only when you intentionally copy reviewed text into another site or export an encrypted file.
        • 
        
      
        
      
        
      There is no account login. The passcode is only for this browser's encrypted vault; the app cannot recover it if it is forgotten.

      Prepare on laptop
      
        
      Pre-Rounding Checklist Builder
      
        
      Paste Epic context, de-identify it, and copy the prompts you need before leaving your laptop.
      
      
      

      
      
        
        
      
      

      
      
        
      Laptop: chart context -> first report -> checklist
      
        
      Phone: checklist -> bedside answers -> final update
      
      
      

      
      
        
          
          
            Local privacy is on
            No app-server upload or analytics by default. Review prompts before sharing externally.
          
          Details
        
        
      
          
      
            
      
              
      Local guardrails
      
              
        
                
      • This app does not upload pasted chart text, census data, or case workspaces to an app server by default.
        • 
                
      • No analytics, telemetry, tracking pixels, or ad scripts are included in this app.
        • 
                
      • Raw pasted chart text is used only for local de-identification in this browser tab, then cleared after review.
        • 
                
      • In census mode, saved de-identified case workspaces and continuity timelines are encrypted in this browser with your local passcode.
        • 
              
      
            
      
            
      
              
      External sharing
      
              
        
                
      • Review every prompt before copying to OpenEvidence or another external tool. This app reduces PHI risk but does not legally certify HIPAA de-identification; institutional policy, approved tool use, and any required Business Associate Agreement still govern.
        • 
                
      • Default de-identification is structured-only, so starting the app and pasting chart text does not download model assets from third-party CDNs.
        • 
                
      • Enhanced model de-ID is an opt-in aid. It may download code/model assets, and the scrubbed preview still requires manual PHI review.
        • 
                
      • External clinical AI sites are separate systems. Do not paste PHI unless your institution has approved the workflow and required agreements are in place.
        • 
              
      
            
      
          
      
          
      
            
      
              
              
            
      
            
      
              
            
      
            
      
          
      
        
      
      
      

      
      
        
      
          
      Ready for phone
      
          
      Ready for phone
      
          
      Local bedside checklist is ready for bedside use. Export the encrypted context if the phone needs it, or keep the checklist on this laptop.
      
          
      
            
            
          
      
        
      
        
        
          
      1. Review the local bedside checklist before leaving the laptop.
        2. 
          
      2. Use this app on the bedside device or export the encrypted context file if needed.
        3. 
          
      3. Document patient answers and exam findings in the checklist.
        4. 
        
      
        
      
          
      
            
      Local bedside checklist preview
      
            Checklist ready
          
      
          
      
            28 Items
            6 Sections
            14 Must-ask
            2 High-risk
          
      
          
        
            
      1. Identity & Situation 2 items
        2. 
            
      2. Symptoms & History 6 items
        3. 
            
      3. DKA-specific Assessment 7 items
        4. 
            
      4. Medical History & Risks 5 items
        5. 
            
      5. Physical Exam 4 items
        6. 
            
      6. Labs & Monitoring 4 items
        7. 
          
      
        
      
        
      
          
      Safety reminder
      
          
      Only reviewed, de-identified context is exported. Re-review bedside findings before any final update.
      
          
      PHI risk reduction, not certification.
      
        
      
        
      
          
          Open OpenEvidence
          
          
        
      
        
      
          
      On iPhone: open this app, import the encrypted context file if needed, then continue the bedside checklist.
      
          
      Use OpenEvidence separately for rounds synthesis, medication safety, teaching, or guideline-dependent questions.
      
        
      
      
      

      
      
        
      
      
      
        
      
          
      Laptop
      
          
      Add chart context
      
          
      Add the note, labs, handoff, or medication record you need for rounds. De-identify locally before copying any prompt.
      
        
      

        
      
          
      OpenEvidence prompts
      
          
      Keep this open after each copy. Use OpenEvidence for rounds synthesis, teaching, medication safety, and guideline-dependent checks; bedside checklists are built locally.
      
          
      
            
      
              
      
                
      OpenEvidence review tasks
      
                
      Choose a task, copy a PHI-reviewed prompt, then paste the answer here for local review and structured summary storage.

      Chart info
      
            
      Paste Epic info
      
            
      Review the scrubbed preview before copying. Raw text is not stored.
      
          
      
          
          
          
          Optional. Paste blood counts, metabolic panels, cultures, imaging impressions, or trends from the chart.
          
          
          Optional. Paste sign-out summaries, overnight events, to-dos, consultant updates, nursing concerns, or details that are not in the subjective/objective/assessment/plan note.
          
          
          Optional chart context. Paste active orders and medication administration record rows if they help explain today's medication questions.

              Opening frame
              
                    Start aloud
                  
              
                  
              What brought you in, and what would you most want the team to understand today?
              
                
              
                
              
                  
              Primary symptom (pick one or more)
              
                  
              
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                    
                  
              
                
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
                    Patient's own words when possible.
                    
                  
              
                  
              
                    
                    Medicine, surgery, neurology, etc.
                    
                  
              
                  
              
                    
                    Use age and sex when possible.
                    
                  
              
                  
              
                    
                    Background plus why they are here.
                    
                  
              
                
              
              
              

              
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              History of present illness story
              
                    Timeline first
                  
              
                  
              When did this start, how has it changed, what makes it better or worse, and what symptoms travel with it?
              
                
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Onset
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Timing
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Associated symptoms (select all that apply)
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                
              
                
              
                  
                  Timing, symptom character, associated symptoms, severity, treatments tried, and patient concerns.
                  
                
              
              
              

              
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              Background and baseline
              
                    Do not skip function
                  
              
                  
              What conditions matter for this admission, what is their baseline, and what prior procedures change today's risk?
              
                
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Past medical history
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Baseline

              Medications and allergies
              
                    Verify changes
                  
              
                  
              What do they actually take, what changed recently, what did they miss, and what reactions have they had?
              
                
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Meds
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Medication classes
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                  
              
                    
              
                      Allergies
                      
              
                        
                        
                        
                        
                      
              
                    
              
                  
              
                
              
                
              
                  
                  Home meds, adherence, recent changes, OTC/supplements, last dose, allergy reaction.
                  
                
              
              
              

              
              
                
              
                  
              
                    
              Social, family, and review of systems
              
                    Function + safety

          More options
          
                  
            
                  
              Expected input format
              
                  BEDSIDE QUESTION CHECKLIST

SYMPTOM TRAJECTORY
Since yesterday, has your weakness changed?: Better / Same / Worse / Other ___
Has numbness or tingling moved higher on your body?: No / Yes, location ___

BREATHING AND SWALLOWING
Are you having any trouble breathing while resting?: No / Yes, mild / Yes, severe / Other ___
Have you had any trouble swallowing food, water, pills, or saliva?: No / Yes / Other ___

TARGETED PHYSICAL EXAM CHECKLIST

VITAL SIGNS AND SUPPORT
Respiratory rate: ___
SpO2: ___

MUSCULOSKELETAL STRENGTH EXAM
Upper extremity strength right: 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5
Upper extremity strength left: 0 / 1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5
